name: Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop from Vinuesa
name_original: Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – circular desde Vinuesa
description: Hike the moderate 10.6-mile Embalse Cuerda del Pozo loop from Vinuesa, enjoying pine forests, reservoir views, and the submerged village of
region_intro: Hiking around Vinuesa, located in the province of Soria, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes shaped by glacial origins. The region features rugged mountains, ancient pine forests, and significant waterways, including the source of the Duero River. Notable natural features include the high-altitude Laguna Negra and the Picos de Urbión, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. This area is characterized by its glacial lakes, such as Laguna Larga and Laguna Helada, and extensive forest cover. Best hiking trails around Vinuesa The most popular hiking route is Pico Urbión and the source of the Duero River – circular from Laguna Negra (Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión), a difficult 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route ascends to Pico Urbión, offering views of glacial cirques and the Duero River's source. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop from Vinuesa, a moderate 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path. This circular route provides views of the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir and surrounding landscapes. Local hikers also love the Waterfall of the Laguna Negra – Laguna Negra de Urbión loop from Laguna Negra de Urbión, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through the area around the iconic Laguna Negra, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Hiking around Vinuesa is defined by glacial lakes, extensive pine forests, and rugged mountain terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Vinuesa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 115 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Vinuesa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Vinuesa?
A: Yes, Vinuesa offers several easy to moderate hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. While the region is known for its challenging mountain ascents, you can find routes that provide a more leisurely experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14938451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Laguna Negra – Laguna Negra de Urbión loop from Laguna Negra de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.1-mile (5 km) trail that takes less than two hours to complete, offering beautiful scenery around the iconic Laguna Negra.

Q: What are the main natural attractions or landmarks I can see while hiking in Vinuesa?
A: Vinuesa's hiking trails lead to stunning natural landmarks shaped by glacial origins. The most prominent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6224900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt;, a glacial lake shrouded in legend. Many routes also offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, the highest peak in the Sierra de Urbión, and the source of the Duero River. You might also encounter other glacial lakes like Laguna Larga and Laguna Helada, as well as extensive pine forests.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Vinuesa?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Vinuesa are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without needing to retrace your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063148243/cuerda-del-pozo-reservoir-loop-from-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop from Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.6-mile (17.1 km) path offering scenic views of the reservoir and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Can I hike with my dog on the trails around Vinuesa?
A: The natural parks and trails around Vinuesa are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Natural Park of Laguna Negra and Glacial Cirques of Urbión, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check specific park regulations before you go.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Vinuesa?
A: The hiking routes in Vinuesa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 115 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the dramatic glacial landscapes, the beauty of the Laguna Negra, and the rewarding views from the Picos de Urbión, often highlighting the sense of ancient wilderness and diverse terrain.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Vinuesa?
A: Yes, for popular areas like the Laguna Negra, there is designated parking available. From the parking area, you can typically access various trails, including the pleasant walk directly to the lagoon or more adventurous ascents towards Pico de Urbión. It's advisable to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or water activities after a hike?
A: While the waters of Laguna Negra are famously cold, it does host a swimming event on the first Sunday of August. The region is rich in water, with several glacial lakes and the Duero River. However, for general wild swimming, always exercise caution, check local regulations, and be aware of water temperatures and currents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vinuesa?
A: The best time to hike in Vinuesa largely depends on your preferred experience. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, ideal for most trails. Summer is popular, especially for higher altitude routes, but can be warm. Winter hiking is possible, particularly around the Laguna Negra, but requires appropriate gear due to snow and cold conditions in the Picos de Urbión.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking paths that pass through or near Vinuesa?
A: Yes, the Vinuesa area is part of the &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sendero_de_Gran_Recorrido" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR-14 Senda del Duero (Duero Path)&lt;/a&gt;, a significant long-distance trail. This path follows the Duero River from its source in the Picos de Urbión through various towns, offering an extensive hiking experience for those looking for multi-day adventures.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Vinuesa?
A: Vinuesa offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 25 distinct trails recorded on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's a path for every type of hiker.

Q: Where can I learn more about the local ecosystem and geography of the area?
A: Between Vinuesa and Laguna Negra, you can visit the Casa del Parque. This interpretive center provides valuable information about the local ecosystem and the unique glacial geography of the Natural Park of Laguna Negra and the Urbión Glacier Circles, enhancing your understanding of the landscapes you'll explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in Vinuesa?
A: The terrain in Vinuesa is highly varied due to its glacial origins. You can expect rugged mountains, extensive ancient pine forests, and areas rich with glacial lakes. Trails range from well-defined forest paths to rocky, challenging ascents in the higher elevations of the Picos de Urbión, offering a diverse hiking experience.
tips: If the water level is high we will not be able to see the Old Roman Bridge of Vinuesa that is hidden under the water. It is worth going when the water level allows us to appreciate such a wonder surrounded by nature.
Beautiful views over the Duero river to the town of Vinuesa from the Los Carbonales viewpoint. We can access by car, since in the same place we will find a parking lot.
The parish church of Nuestra Señora del Pino is Gothic-Renaissance in style. Construction began in 1591 by the master stonemason Juan de Naveda (author of one of the Escorial towers).

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo circular hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Vinuesa. You can typically find parking within the village or near the access points to the reservoir from Vinuesa. Given its popularity, especially in summer, arriving earlier in the day is advisable to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially around recreational areas like Playa Pita and when passing through populated sections or near wildlife. Always carry bags to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is typically from spring to autumn. During these seasons, the weather is pleasant, and the scenery is vibrant. While possible in winter, be prepared for colder temperatures and potential snow, especially on higher ground, which can affect trail conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this trail is generally easily accessible, consisting of well-defined paths. You'll walk through extensive pine forests and along the reservoir's banks. Expect a mix of dirt paths and some sections that might be stony, but nothing overly technical.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience diverse scenery around the Cuerda del Pozo reservoir. Notable features include the stunning views of the reservoir itself, often called 'the freshwater sea of Soria,' and the lush pine forests. You might also spot the unique, sometimes visible, church bell tower of the submerged village of La Muedra, especially during periods of drought. Highlights near Vinuesa include the Ancient Roman Bridge of Vinuesa, the View of Vinuesa, and the Parish of Our Lady of the Pine.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the entire circular route around the Embalse Cuerda del Pozo offers numerous scenic viewpoints, particularly overlooking the reservoir and the surrounding pine forests. The View of Vinuesa is a specific highlight offering panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You will encounter parts of the Ruta del Duero, Etapa 01: Fuentes del Duero – Vinuesa, and Etapa 02: Vinuesa – Soria, as well as smaller local paths like Calle del Lavadero.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to Vinuesa?
faqs.answer: While Vinuesa is accessible by road, public transport options might be limited, especially for direct access to the trailhead. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from larger nearby towns like Soria if you plan to rely on public transport.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is natural, you'll find facilities like restrooms and potentially water sources in Vinuesa at the start/end of the hike. Recreational areas around the reservoir, such as Playa Pita, may also offer seasonal amenities. It's always best to carry sufficient water for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing appropriate hiking footwear, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also useful, though the paths are generally well-defined.

waypoints.name: Parish of Our Lady of the Pine
